meta.name: performance-optimization
meta.title: Performance Optimization Skill
meta.description: The most comprehensive, practical, and engineer-authored performance optimization instructions for all languages, frameworks, and stacks. Covers frontend, backend, and database best practices with act

original_definition.skill_description: The most comprehensive, practical, and engineer-authored performance optimization instructions for all languages, frameworks, and stacks. Covers frontend, backend, and database best practices with act

actions.name: Minimize DOM Manipulations: Batch updates where possible. Fr
actions.description: Minimize DOM Manipulations: Batch updates where possible. Frequent DOM changes are expensive.

actions.name: *Anti-pattern:* Updating the DOM in a loop. Instead, build a
actions.description: *Anti-pattern:* Updating the DOM in a loop. Instead, build a document fragment and append it once.

actions.name: *React Example:* Use `React.memo`, `useMemo`, and `useCallba
actions.description: *React Example:* Use `React.memo`, `useMemo`, and `useCallback` to prevent unnecessary renders.

actions.name: Defer Non-Critical Rendering: Use `requestIdleCallback` or s
actions.description: Defer Non-Critical Rendering: Use `requestIdleCallback` or similar to defer work until the browser is idle.

actions.name: Image Compression: Use tools like ImageOptim, Squoosh, or Ti
actions.description: Image Compression: Use tools like ImageOptim, Squoosh, or TinyPNG. Prefer modern formats (WebP, AVIF) for web delivery.

actions.name: Minification and Bundling: Use Webpack, Rollup, or esbuild t
actions.description: Minification and Bundling: Use Webpack, Rollup, or esbuild to bundle and minify JS/CSS. Enable tree-shaking to remove dead code.

actions.name: Cache Headers: Set long-lived cache headers for static asset
actions.description: Cache Headers: Set long-lived cache headers for static assets. Use cache busting for updates.

actions.name: Lazy Loading: Use `loading="lazy"` for images, and dynamic i
actions.description: Lazy Loading: Use `loading="lazy"` for images, and dynamic imports for JS modules/components.

actions.name: Preload and Prefetch: Use `<link rel="preload">` and `<link
actions.description: Preload and Prefetch: Use `<link rel="preload">` and `<link rel="prefetch">` for critical resources.

actions.name: Use `trackBy` in `ngFor` for efficient list rendering.
actions.description: Use `trackBy` in `ngFor` for efficient list rendering.

actions.name: Lazy load modules and components with the Angular Router.
actions.description: Lazy load modules and components with the Angular Router.

actions.name: Lazy load components and routes with Vue Router.
actions.description: Lazy load components and routes with Vue Router.

actions.name: Loading large JS bundles on initial page load.
actions.description: Loading large JS bundles on initial page load.

actions.name: Not compressing images or using outdated formats.
actions.description: Not compressing images or using outdated formats.

actions.name: Ignoring mobile performance (test on real devices!).
actions.description: Ignoring mobile performance (test on real devices!).

rules.description: Virtual DOM Frameworks: Use React, Vue, or similar efficiently—avoid unnecessary re-renders.
rules.name: Virtual DOM Frameworks: Use React, Vue,...

rules.description: Keys in Lists: Always use stable keys in lists to help virtual DOM diffing. Avoid using array indices as keys unless the list is static.

rules.description: Avoid Inline Styles: Inline styles can trigger layout thrashing. Prefer CSS classes.

rules.description: Defer/Async Scripts: Use `defer` or `async` for non-critical JS to avoid blocking rendering.

rules.description: Avoid Blocking the Main Thread: Offload heavy computation to Web Workers.
rules.name: Avoid Blocking the Main Thread: Offload...

rules.description: Debounce/Throttle Events: For scroll, resize, and input events, use debounce/throttle to limit handler frequency.
rules.name: Debounce/Throttle Events: For scroll, resize, and...

rules.description: Memory Leaks: Clean up event listeners, intervals, and DOM references. Use browser dev tools to check for detached nodes.
rules.name: Memory Leaks: Clean up event listeners,...

rules.description: Avoid Global Variables: Globals can cause memory leaks and unpredictable performance.

rules.description: Avoid Deep Object Cloning: Use shallow copies or libraries like lodash's `cloneDeep` only when necessary.

directives.description: Measure First, Optimize Second: Always profile and measure before optimizing. Use benchmarks, profilers, and monitoring tools to identify real bottlen
directives.name: Measure First, Optimize Second: Always profile...

directives.description: *Pro Tip:* Use tools like Chrome DevTools, Lighthouse, New Relic, Datadog, Py-Spy, or your language's built-in profilers.
directives.name: *Pro Tip:* Use tools like Chrome...

directives.description: Optimize for the Common Case: Focus on optimizing code paths that are most frequently executed. Don't waste time on rare edge cases unless they're cri
directives.name: Optimize for the Common Case: Focus...

directives.description: Avoid Premature Optimization: Write clear, maintainable code first; optimize only when necessary. Premature optimization can make code harder to read
directives.name: Avoid Premature Optimization: Write clear, maintainable...

directives.description: Minimize Resource Usage: Use memory, CPU, network, and disk resources efficiently. Always ask: "Can this be done with less?"
directives.name: Minimize Resource Usage: Use memory, CPU,...

directives.description: Prefer Simplicity: Simple algorithms and data structures are often faster and easier to optimize. Don't over-engineer.
directives.name: Prefer Simplicity: Simple algorithms and data...

directives.name: Document Performance Assumptions: Clearly comment on...

directives.description: Understand the Platform: Know the performance characteristics of your language, framework, and runtime. What's fast in Python may be slow in JavaScrip
directives.name: Understand the Platform: Know the performance...

directives.description: Automate Performance Testing: Integrate performance tests and benchmarks into your CI/CD pipeline. Catch regressions early.

directives.description: Set Performance Budgets: Define acceptable limits for load time, memory usage, API latency, etc. Enforce them with automated checks.
